summary:
The lower lip usually rests directly on the Ricketts' esthetic plane during rest. This alignment ensures a natural appearance when at ease without protruding forward or backward excessively.
The exact placement helps maintain facial symmetry and aesthetic harmony by keeping lips centered over underlying structures like teeth and jawbone contours as intended under Rickets' principles. To summarize concisely while focusing only on critical aspects related to your query about where the lower lip should ideally sit according to established dental aesthetics guidelines based primarily around Dr.Rickett’s work,
